mission.title: Install and Run xRegistry
mission.description: xRegistry is a CNCF Sandbox metadata registry for discovering, validating, and versioning resources in distributed systems. It provides both a server (API) and a CLI tool (`xr`). The server runs as a Docker container with an embedded MySQL database — no Kubernetes manifests are provided by the project.

steps.title: Run xRegistry server with Docker
steps.description:
Start the xRegistry server with an embedded MySQL database and sample data:
```bash
docker run -ti -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/xregistry/xrserver-all --samples
```
The `--samples` flag preloads sample Registries for exploration. Omit it to start with an empty Registry.

When ready, the API server will be available at `http://localhost:8080`.

steps.title: Verify the server is running
steps.description:
Test the API with curl:
```bash
curl http://localhost:8080
```
Expected output includes `specversion`, `registryid`, and `self` fields.

You can also access the browser-based explorer at:
```
http://localhost:8080?ui
```

steps.title: Install the xRegistry CLI
steps.description:
Download the standalone `xr` CLI from the GitHub releases:
```bash
# macOS (Apple Silicon)
curl -Lo xr https://github.com/xregistry/server/releases/download/dev/xr-darwin-arm64
chmod +x xr
sudo mv xr /usr/local/bin/

# Linux (amd64)
curl -Lo xr https://github.com/xregistry/server/releases/download/dev/xr-linux-amd64
chmod +x xr
sudo mv xr /usr/local/bin/
```
See all available binaries at https://github.com/xregistry/server/releases/tag/dev

steps.title: Explore sample data
steps.description:
If you started the server with `--samples`, explore the preloaded registries:
```bash
# List the DocStore registry
curl http://localhost:8080/reg-DocStore

# Browse in the UI
open http://localhost:8080/reg-DocStore?ui
```
See the [`samples/doc-store`](https://github.com/xregistry/server/tree/main/samples/doc-store) script for additional setup with sample data.

uninstall.title: Stop the xRegistry container
uninstall.description:
Stop the running Docker container:
```bash
docker ps | grep xrserver
docker stop <container-id>
```

uninstall.title: Remove the CLI
uninstall.description:
Remove the xr CLI binary:
```bash
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/xr
```

upgrade.title: Pull the latest image
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest xRegistry server image:
```bash
docker pull ghcr.io/xregistry/xrserver-all
```

upgrade.title: Restart with the new image
upgrade.description:
Stop the current container and start a new one:
```bash
docker stop <container-id>
docker run -ti -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/xregistry/xrserver-all --samples
```

troubleshooting.title: Port 8080 already in use
troubleshooting.description:
If port 8080 is in use, map to a different port:
```bash
docker run -ti -p 9090:8080 ghcr.io/xregistry/xrserver-all --samples
```
Then access the server at `http://localhost:9090`.

troubleshooting.title: Container exits immediately
troubleshooting.description:
Check the container logs for errors:
```bash
docker logs <container-id>
```
Ensure Docker has enough memory allocated (the embedded MySQL requires at least 512MB).

resolution.summary: A successful installation will have the xRegistry server running as a Docker container, accessible at http://localhost:8080 (API) and http://localhost:8080?ui (browser explorer). The xr CLI can be used to manage data in Registries.

prerequisites.description: Docker must be installed and running. No Kubernetes cluster is required — xRegistry runs as a standalone Docker container.
